title: Cytotechnologist
description: AstraZeneca's Oncology Division seeks a Cytotechnologist to examine cellular specimens and contribute to drug development research. Working remotely from anywhere in the US, you'll analyze histopathology and cytology samples using advanced laboratory techniques, prepare microscopy reports, and support clinical trial pathology. This role requires expertise in identifying cellular abnormalities and a commitment to precision in diagnostic work.
responsibilities: Prepare, stain, and examine tissue and body fluid specimens using microscopy techniques
Identify and document cellular abnormalities and morphological changes under supervision
Maintain detailed laboratory records and quality assurance documentation
Collaborate with pathologists and research scientists on specimen analysis and interpretation
Perform special staining procedures and immunohistochemical techniques as needed
Adhere to strict protocol standards for research-grade specimen handling and analysis

requirements.education: Associate degree in cytotechnology or related field; Bachelor's degree preferred
certifications: ASCP certification or eligible to obtain

benefits: Health, dental, vision, and mental health coverage
Professional development and continuing education support
